statement.text: (8) Where this Regulation provides for specifications or restrictions of its rules by Member State law, Member States may, as far as necessary for coherence and for making the national provisions comprehensible to the persons to whom they apply, incorporate elements of this Regulation into their national law.

classification_rationale: This recital provides non-binding guidance to Member States on incorporating Regulation elements, not a binding rule, definition, or scope.

condition.value: for Member States to specify its rules

classification_rationale: This statement describes the Regulation's design allowing Member States to specify rules, defining a scope for national law.
anchor: This Regulation also provides a margin of manoeuvre for Member States to specify its rules
profile_gate_override: hc=True overridden to False: no MigrainePredict profile dimension matched in the scoped text

condition.value: that sets out the circumstances for specific processing situations, including determining more precisely the conditions under which the processing of personal data is lawful

classification_rationale: This statement clarifies that the Regulation does not preclude certain Member State laws, defining a scope for national legislation.
anchor: this Regulation does not exclude Member State law that sets out the circumstances for specific processing situations

statement.text: (10) In order to ensure a consistent and high level of protection of natural persons and to remove the obstacles to flows of personal data within the Union, the level of protection of the rights and freedoms of natural persons with regard to the processing of such data should be equivalent in all Member States. Consistent and homogenous application of the rules for the protection of the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data should be ensured throughout the Union. Regarding the processing of personal data for compliance with a legal obligation, for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller, Member States should be allowed to maintain or introduce national provisions to further specify the application of the rules of this Regulation. In conjunction with the general and horizontal law on data protection implementing Directive 95/46/EC, Member States have several sector-specific laws in areas that need more specific provisions. This Regulation also provides a margin of manoeuvre for Member States to specify its rules, including for the processing of special categories of personal data (‘sensitive data’). To that extent, this Regulation does not exclude Member State law that sets out the circumstances for specific processing situations, including determining more precisely the conditions under which the processing of personal data is lawful.

classification_rationale: This statement expresses a general objective or interpretive guidance, not a binding rule or scope condition. | This statement is a factual observation about existing legal frameworks, not a binding rule or scope condition.

statement.text: (13) In order to ensure a consistent level of protection for natural persons throughout the Union and to prevent divergences hampering the free movement of personal data within the internal market, a Regulation is necessary to provide legal certainty and transparency for economic operators, including micro, small and medium-sized enterprises, and to provide natural persons in all Member States with the same level of legally enforceable rights and obligations and responsibilities for controllers and processors, to ensure consistent monitoring of the processing of personal data, and equivalent sanctions in all Member States as well as effective cooperation between the supervisory authorities of different Member States. The proper functioning of the internal market requires that the free movement of personal data within the Union is not restricted or prohibited for reasons connected with the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data. To take account of the specific situation of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises, this Regulation includes a derogation for organisations with fewer than 250 employees with regard to record-keeping. In addition, the Union institutions and bodies, and Member States and their supervisory authorities, are encouraged to take account of the specific needs of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises in the application of this Regulation. The notion of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises should draw from Article 2 of the Annex to Commission Recommendation 2003/361/EC ( 5 ) .

classification_rationale: This statement describes the purpose and necessity of the Regulation, which is motivational preamble. | This statement outlines a foundational principle for the internal market, serving as motivational preamble. | This recital describes a feature of the Regulation, but does not itself establish a binding rule or definition. | This statement provides non-binding interpretive guidance, using 'encouraged' in a recital. | This is a pointer-only reference to an external source for a notion, not a definition within the Regulation.

condition.value: whatever their nationality or place of residence

classification_rationale: This statement defines the personal scope of the Regulation's protection.
anchor: The protection afforded by this Regulation should apply to natural persons, whatever their nationality or place of residence

condition.value: which concerns legal persons and in particular undertakings established as legal persons, including the name and the form of the legal person and the contact details of the legal person

condition.value: by automated means, as well as to manual processing, if the personal data are contained or are intended to be contained in a filing system

classification_rationale: This statement describes the material scope of the Regulation's protection based on processing methods and data organization.
anchor: The protection of natural persons should apply to the processing of personal data by automated means, as well as to manual processing, if the personal data are contained or are intended to be contained in a filing system

applies_to.value: Files or sets of files, as well as their cover pages

condition.value: which are not structured according to specific criteria

classification_rationale: This statement specifies an exclusion from the material scope of the Regulation based on data structure.
anchor: Files or sets of files... which are not structured according to specific criteria should not fall within the scope of this Regulation
profile_gate_override: hc=True overridden to False: EXCLUDES-polarity scope is a carve-out, not in-scope

statement.text: (15) In order to prevent creating a serious risk of circumvention, the protection of natural persons should be technologically neutral and should not depend on the techniques used. The protection of natural persons should apply to the processing of personal data by automated means, as well as to manual processing, if the personal data are contained or are intended to be contained in a filing system. Files or sets of files, as well as their cover pages, which are not structured according to specific criteria should not fall within the scope of this Regulation.

classification_rationale: This statement provides general interpretive guidance, which is not binding in a recital.

condition.value: in the context of the activities of an establishment of a controller or a processor in the Union

classification_rationale: This paragraph defines the material and personal scope of the Regulation based on the establishment of a controller or processor.
anchor: This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data in the context of the activities of an establishment of a controller or a processor in the Union

definition.value: any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person (‘data subject’)

classification_rationale: This statement defines the regulatory term 'personal data'.
anchor: 'personal data' means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person

definition.value: an identifiable natural person is one who can be identified, directly or indirectly, in particular by reference to an identifier such as a name, an identification number, location data, an online identifier or to one or more factors specific to the physical, physiological, genetic, mental, economic, cultural or social identity of that natural person

classification_rationale: This statement defines the regulatory term 'identifiable natural person'.
anchor: an identifiable natural person is one who can be identified, directly or indirectly, in particular by reference to an identifier

definition.value: any operation or set of operations which is performed on personal data or on sets of personal data, whether or not by automated means, such as collection, recording, organisation, structuring, storage, adaptation or alteration, retrieval, consultation, use, disclosure by transmission, dissemination or otherwise making available, alignment or combination, restriction, erasure or destruction

classification_rationale: This paragraph provides the intensional definition of the term 'processing' for the Regulation.
anchor: ‘processing’ means any operation or set of operations which is performed on personal data

definition.value: personal data resulting from specific technical processing relating to the physical, physiological or behavioural characteristics of a natural person, which allow or confirm the unique identification of that natural person, such as facial images or dactyloscopic data

classification_rationale: This paragraph defines the regulatory term 'biometric data' by specifying its characteristics and scope.
anchor: ‘biometric data’ means personal data resulting from specific technical processing relating to characteristics of a natural person

definition.value: personal data related to the physical or mental health of a natural person, including the provision of health care services, which reveal information about his or her health status

classification_rationale: This paragraph provides the definition for the regulatory term 'data concerning health'.
anchor: 'data concerning health' means personal data related to the physical or mental health of a natural person

predicate.value: process lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner

classification_rationale: This paragraph establishes an obligation for how personal data shall be processed.
anchor: processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject

object.value: personal data for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes

classification_rationale: This statement imposes an obligation on how personal data must be collected.
anchor: collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes

condition.value: in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes

classification_rationale: This statement imposes a prohibition on further processing of personal data.
anchor: not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes

predicate.value: not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes

object.value: further processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes

classification_rationale: This statement provides a dispensation from the incompatibility prohibition under specific conditions.
anchor: further processing for archiving purposes... shall... not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes
predicate_negation_contradiction: not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes

object.value: processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, or trade union membership, and the processing of genetic data, biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation

classification_rationale: This paragraph establishes a clear prohibition on processing specific categories of personal data.
anchor: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin... or sexual orientation shall be prohibited.

condition.value: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, for the assessment of the working capacity of the employee, medical diagnosis, the provision of health or social care or treatment or the management of health or social care systems and services on the basis of Union or Member State law or pursuant to contract with a health professional and subject to the conditions and safeguards referred to in paragraph 3

classification_rationale: This paragraph specifies a condition under which the processing of special categories of personal data is permitted, acting as an exception to a prohibition.
anchor: processing is necessary for the purposes of preventive or occupational medicine, medical diagnosis, health or social care

definition.value: a machine-based system that is designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment, and that, for explicit or implicit objectives, infers, from the input it receives, how to generate outputs such as predictions, content, recommendations, or decisions that can influence physical or virtual environments

classification_rationale: This paragraph provides the definition for the regulatory term 'AI system'.
anchor: 'AI system' means a machine-based system that is designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy

condition.value: where both of the following conditions are fulfilled

classification_rationale: This paragraph establishes the conditions under which an AI system falls within the material scope of "high-risk" by reference to subsequent points.
anchor: that AI system shall be considered to be high-risk where both of the following conditions are fulfilled

condition.value: pursuant to Article 6(2) are the AI systems listed in any of the following areas

classification_rationale: This paragraph establishes the material scope for high-risk AI systems by introducing a list of categories.
anchor: High-risk AI systems pursuant to Article 6(2) are the AI systems listed in any of the following areas
